req.title: Timezone handling
req.statement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC; UI shall render in user's configured timezone.
error_pathways: default to UTC when tz missing

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-108 mandates UTC storage with UI rendering in the user's configured timezone. Three distinct test scenarios are required: (1) EP positive – a user with a valid non-UTC timezone (e.g., America/New_York) sees timestamps converted correctly; (2) NEG negative – a user with no configured timezone sees timestamps defaulted to UTC per the error pathway; (3) BVA boundary – a timestamp at the exact UTC midnight boundary (00:00:00Z) is rendered in a timezone that shifts it to the previous calendar day (e.g., UTC-5 renders it as 19:00 the prior day), validating date-rollover arithmetic. Each test authenticates programmatically, seeds a known UTC timestamp, and asserts the rendered text without side effects.

tests.name: test_timestamp_rendered_in_user_configured_timezone
preconditions: User profile has timezone set to 'America/New_York' (UTC-5 / UTC-4 DST)
A resource with a known UTC timestamp exists in the system
actions: Login as user with America/New_York timezone
Navigate to a screen that displays the seeded UTC timestamp
Read the rendered timestamp text from the UI
expected: Rendered timestamp reflects America/New_York offset (UTC-5 or UTC-4 depending on DST)
UTC value stored in DB is unchanged
No UTC suffix visible in the displayed string
trace: R-WEB-108: UI shall render in user's configured timezone
R-WEB-108: Timestamps stored shall be UTC
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
import pytz

UTC_TIMESTAMP = "2024-03-15T14:00:00Z"
USER_TZ = "America/New_York"

@pytest.fixture
def ny_user_session(<auth_fixture>):
    return <auth_fixture>.login_with_tz(USER_TZ)

def test_timestamp_rendered_in_user_configured_timezone(ny_user_session, <page_fixture>):
    resource_id = <seed_utc_timestamp>(UTC_TIMESTAMP)
    page = <page_fixture>(ny_user_session)
    page.goto(f"/resources/{resource_id}")

    rendered = page.locator("[data-testid='timestamp']").inner_text()

    utc_dt = datetime(2024, 3, 15, 14, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    tz = pytz.timezone(USER_TZ)
    expected_local = utc_dt.astimezone(tz)
    expected_str = expected_local.strftime("%H:%M")

    assert expected_str in rendered, (
        f"Expected local time {expected_str} in rendered '{rendered}'"
    )
    assert "UTC" not in rendered

    <cleanup_resource>(resource_id)

tests.name: test_timestamp_defaults_to_utc_when_timezone_missing
preconditions: User profile has NO configured timezone (null/empty)
A resource with a known UTC timestamp exists in the system
actions: Login as user whose profile timezone field is null
Navigate to a screen displaying the seeded UTC timestamp
Read the rendered timestamp text from the UI
expected: Rendered timestamp matches the raw UTC time (error pathway: default to UTC)
System does not throw an error or display a broken timestamp
trace: R-WEB-108: Error pathway – default to UTC when tz missing

tests.name: test_timestamp_at_utc_midnight_boundary_renders_previous_day_in_negative_offset_tz
preconditions: User profile has timezone set to 'America/Chicago' (UTC-6 standard)
A resource with UTC timestamp exactly at 00:00:00Z is seeded
actions: Login as user with America/Chicago timezone
Navigate to a screen displaying the UTC midnight timestamp
Read both the date portion and time portion of the rendered timestamp
expected: Rendered date is one day prior to the UTC date (day rollover)
Rendered time is 18:00 (UTC 00:00 minus 6 hours = previous day 18:00)
trace: R-WEB-108: UI shall render in user's configured timezone
R-WEB-108: BVA – UTC midnight is the boundary where date changes under negative offsets
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
import pytz

UTC_MIDNIGHT = "2024-11-15T00:00:00Z"
USER_TZ = "America/Chicago"

@pytest.fixture
def chicago_user_session(<auth_fixture>):
    return <auth_fixture>.login_with_tz(USER_TZ)

def test_timestamp_at_utc_midnight_boundary_renders_previous_day_in_negative_offset_tz(
    chicago_user_session, <page_fixture>
):
    resource_id = <seed_utc_timestamp>(UTC_MIDNIGHT)
    page = <page_fixture>(chicago_user_session)
    page.goto(f"/resources/{resource_id}")

    rendered = page.locator("[data-testid='timestamp']").inner_text()

    utc_dt = datetime(2024, 11, 15, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    tz = pytz.timezone(USER_TZ)
    local_dt = utc_dt.astimezone(tz)

    assert str(local_dt.day) in rendered, "Expected previous day in rendered output"
    assert local_dt.strftime("%H:%M") in rendered, (
        f"Expected {local_dt.strftime('%H:%M')} in '{rendered}'"
    )
    assert "15" not in rendered.split()[0], "UTC date 15 must not appear as local date"

    <cleanup_resource>(resource_id)
